evidence: SemanticFieldExplorer.java line 268-272: `int minNounsForCore = Math.max(2, discoveredNouns.size() / 3)` then `if (freq >= minNounsForCore || freq >= 2)` — since minNounsForCore is always >= 2, the first disjunct is always subsumed by `freq >= 2`; minNounsForCore is never the effective threshold.

ExplorationHandlers.java lines 172-176: `HttpApiUtils.requireParam` already returns null for empty/blank values (trims and checks isEmpty before returning); the subsequent `if (nounsParam.isBlank())` guard is therefore always false — dead code after a non-null return.
BlackLabConllUIndexer.java lines 63-65: `e.getMessage()` is called unconditionally before `if (e != null)`, proving e is non-null at the check point; the null guard is always-true dead code.
Guard clauses are applied consistently across all HTTP handlers (null-return pattern via requireParam, early return after sendError).
BlackLabQueryExecutor uses a three-phase pipeline (collect, score, rank) with clear phase boundaries documented in Javadoc.
CqlUtils.splitCqlTokens, GrammarConfigLoader.deriveTokenPosition, and BlackLabSnippetParser.findLabelPosition each use explicit depth-counting bracket parsers with no dead branches.
ComparisonResult filter predicates (getFullyShared, getPartiallyShared, getSpecific) together cover all integer ranges without overlap gaps.

strengths: Guard clauses applied uniformly across all HTTP handlers: requireParam + null-return pattern ensures control flow exits cleanly before any business logic runs.
Three-phase pipeline in executeBcqlQuery (collectHits → scoreHits → rank/limit) is clearly decomposed; each phase has a single responsibility with matching Javadoc.
Bracket-depth parsers in CqlUtils.splitCqlTokens and BlackLabSnippetParser.findLabelPosition correctly handle all nesting and quoting edge cases without dead branches.
ComparisonResult category predicates (fullyShared / partiallyShared / specific) cover all integer-valued presentInCount outcomes without overlapping conditions.
logic_clarity.issue_character: Issues are isolated logic inconsistencies — one dead compound condition that silently voids an adaptive threshold (identifyCoreCollocates), one always-false guard that is dead code after a stronger check (handleSemanticField), and one post-dereference null check — rather than a systemic pattern of confused control flow.
logic_clarity.score_rationale: The codebase mostly does what it claims: control flow is direct, guard patterns are consistent, and complex parsing logic is correctly implemented. However, the dead `|| freq >= 2` disjunct in identifyCoreCollocates is a meaningful logic clarity failure — it silently makes the dynamically computed threshold irrelevant, so the code implies adaptive behavior but always applies a fixed floor of 2. The always-false isBlank check in handleSemanticField is textbook dead code that mildly obscures the real postcondition of requireParam. Together these push the score below the 'solid but uneven' (80) band toward the mid-70s; the issues are not widespread but the dynamic-threshold defect has real algorithmic implications.

issues.summary: identifyCoreCollocates: `|| freq >= 2` makes the dynamically computed `minNounsForCore` threshold permanently dead
evidence: Line 272: `if (freq >= minNounsForCore || freq >= 2)` — since minNounsForCore is always >= 2, any freq that satisfies minNounsForCore also satisfies freq >= 2; conversely, any freq >= 2 that does NOT satisfy minNounsForCore still passes because of the second disjunct.
Net effect: the condition is always equivalent to `freq >= 2` regardless of corpus size, making the adaptive threshold code a no-op.
issues.suggestion: Remove the `|| freq >= 2` disjunct to let the dynamic threshold operate as intended: `if (freq >= minNounsForCore)`. If the fixed floor of 2 is genuinely desired as a safety net, document it explicitly and verify the intent, e.g. `int effectiveThreshold = Math.max(2, minNounsForCore); if (freq >= effectiveThreshold)` — but note this is identical to the current wrong behavior and only has different semantics when minNounsForCore < 2, which can never happen.

issues.summary: handleSemanticField: `nounsParam.isBlank()` guard is always false after `requireParam` already rejects blank inputs
evidence: HttpApiUtils.requireParam (line 627-632): trims the value and returns null (sending 400) if isEmpty(); non-null return guarantees a non-empty, trimmed string.
ExplorationHandlers line 172-176: after `if (nounsParam == null) return;`, the value is provably non-blank, so `if (nounsParam.isBlank())` on line 175 can never be true.
issues.suggestion: Remove the redundant `if (nounsParam.isBlank())` block (lines 175-178). The requireParam null-check on line 173 already covers this case. If the intent was to handle a blank-after-trim case differently, note that requireParam already sends a 400 for that.

issues.summary: BlackLabConllUIndexer.errorOccurred: null check on `e` is dead code because `e.getMessage()` was already called on the previous line
evidence: Line 63: `logger.error("Indexing error in {}: {}", path, e.getMessage());` — dereferences `e` unconditionally.
Line 64: `if (e != null)` — this check is trivially true at this point; if e were null, an NPE would already have been thrown at line 63.
issues.suggestion: Remove the `if (e != null)` guard: `logger.error("Stack trace:", e);` is safe to call unconditionally here. If the BlackLab IndexListener contract actually permits null throwables, move the null check to before line 63 and guard `e.getMessage()` as well (e.g. use `e != null ? e.getMessage() : "<no message>"`).

root_causes: The `|| freq >= 2` pattern in identifyCoreCollocates likely originated as a safety floor during development but was never removed after the dynamic threshold was added; the two conditions were never checked for subsumption.

The isBlank dead code in handleSemanticField likely predates the requireParam abstraction — the check was added before requireParam existed (or was copied from a context where requireParam was not used) and was never cleaned up.
likely_symptoms: dead_isblank_after_requireparam — isolated handler-level dead code, no observable runtime difference
post_dereference_null_check_errorOccurred — logging-only path, no observable runtime difference
